title: Twin Pregnancy - Dichorionic Diamniotic Twins
chief_complaint: 35-year-old G1P0 at 12 weeks with twin pregnancy on ultrasound. Dichorionic diamniotic (DCDA) twins. Conceived via IVF (2 embryos transferred). Mild morning sickness.

physical_exam.abdomen: Uterus larger than dates, 14 weeks size

hidden_data.us_12week: DCDA twins, both CRL consistent with 12 weeks, NT normal, nasal bones present

key_learning_points: DCDA = lowest risk twin pregnancy (separate placentas + sacs)
Chorionicity determines monitoring and delivery timing
Increased risk: preeclampsia, GDM, preterm labor, IUGR

feedback.incorrect_message: This is a DCDA twin pregnancy. The US findings confirm it.
feedback.attending_note: DCDA twins: Serial growth scans q4 weeks. Increased iron (60-100mg) and folic acid (1mg). Screen for GDM and preeclampsia. Deliver at 38 weeks. Vaginal delivery possible if presenting twin cephalic.
